title: Analysis of positron emission scans using descriptors based on fractal analysis
abstract: The present disclosure is directed to systems and methods for the analysis of positron emission scans, or images to show or determine a level of complexity of a surface or structure within the scans to determine a clinical status of a subject, for example to identify, classify and/or grade a disease state or a disease progression of the subject. In an embodiment, the level of complexity can be determined using descriptors based on fractal analysis. In any one or more of the embodiments the disease state can be Alzheimer's disease, the disease progression can be mild cognitive impairment (MCI) progression to dementia or both.
claims:
Claims Therefore, the following is claimed: 1 . A method for radiotracer imaging, comprising: administering a radiotracer to a subject; positioning the subject in relation to an imaging scanner; acquiring one or more radiotracer images from one or more regions of interest of the subject with the imaging scanner; analyzing the one or more images and extracting one or more spatial features from the one or more images relating to distribution of the radiotracer in the one or more regions of interest to show or determine a level of complexity of one or more surfaces within the one or more regions of interest; using the shown or determined level of complexity to determine a clinical status of the subject; and outputting the clinical status of the subject. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the radiotracer is one or more of 11 C-Pittsburgh- Compound-B (PiB), 18 F-flutemetamol, 18 F-florbetapir, or 18 F-florbetaben. 3. The method of any of claims 1 -2, wherein the target activity of the radiotracer in the subject is about 185 MBq -/+ 10% or is at clinical levels. 4. The method of any of claims 1 -3, wherein the imaging scanner is a positron emission tomography scanner. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, further comprising pre-processing the acquired one or more radiotracer images after acquiring the radiotracer images and before analyzing the one or more images. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the pre-processing comprises one or more of averaging the acquired radiotracer images, reorienting the acquired radiotracer images, mapping the image onto a template image space or standard image space. 8. The method of any of claims 1 -7 wherein the one or more spatial features relate to one or more morphological features comprising one or more surfaces defined by the contour of the radiotracer uptake. 9. The method of any of claims 1-8, wherein the one or more spatial features relating to the radiotracer distribution is a Fractal Dimension (FD), Shannon Entropy (SE), or both. 10. The method of any of claims 1 -9, further comprising: positioning the subject in relation to a magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) scanner; acquiring one or more MRI images from the one or more regions of interest of the subject; processing the one or more acquired MRI images; extracting one or more MRI morphological features from the processed MRI image(s); combining into a data set one or more MRI morphological features with one or more spatial features from the radiotracer distribution; and using the combined features to show or determine a clinical status of the subject. 1 1 . The method of claim 10, wherein the one or more MRI morphological features are one or more of cortical thickness, lateral ventrical volume, or hippocampal volume, individually or in combination. 12. The method of claim 10, further comprising the segmentation of white and grey matter regions from the MRI and computation of one or more spatial features from the contour of the segmented MRI including, but not limited to, Fractal Dimension (FD), Shannon Entropy (SE), or both. 13. The method of any of claims 1 -12, further comprising: inputting a data set of the extracted one or more spatial features relating to distribution of the radiotracer or the combined data set into a classifier to identify, classify, and/or grade one or more disease states, one or more disease progressions, or both; and outputting an identification, classification and/or grading of one or more disease states of, one or more disease progressions, or both. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the classifier is a support vector machine (SVM). 15. The method of any of claims 1 -14, wherein one of the one or more disease states is Alzheimerâs disease. 16. The method of any of claims 1 -15, wherein one of the one or more disease progressions is mild cognitive impairment (MCI) progression to dementia. 17. A system, comprising: a positron emission tomography (PET) imaging scanner; at least one computing device having a processor and a memory; and at least one application executable in the at least one computing device stored in the memory that, upon positioning the imaging scanner in relation to a subject and setting a labeling plane within one or more regions of interest in a subject in which the scanner detects radiotracer uptake, when executed by the processor, the application causes the computing device to at least: acquire one or more radiotracer images from one or more regions of interest of the subject with the imaging scanner, the subject having been administered a radiotracer; analyze the one or more images and extract one or more spatial features relating to the radiotracer distribution to show or determine a level of complexity of one or more surfaces or structures within the image(s), wherein the one or more spatial features relating to the radiotracer distribution is a Fractal Dimension (FD), Shannon Entropy (SE), or both; use the shown or determined level of complexity to determine a clinical status of the subject; and output the clinical status of the subject. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein the radiotracer is one or more of 11 C-Pittsburgh- Compound-B (PiB), 18 F-flutemetamol, 18 F-florbetapir, or 18 F-florbetaben. 19.
